text: this is 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English. I'm Phil.
zh: 这里是BBC英语教学的《六分钟英语》节目。我是Phil。

text: And I'm Beth. A list is a collection of items with a single item written on each line.
zh: 我是Beth。清单就是把若干项目一项一项分行写下来的东西。

text: And they're everywhere. Shopping lists, to -do lists, even bucket lists.
zh: 清单无处不在。购物清单、待办事项清单,甚至遗愿清单。

text: A list of all the things a person would like to do before they die.
zh: 遗愿清单是指一个人在去世前想要完成的所有事情的清单。

text: In fact, there's an episode of 6 Minute English all about them.
zh: 实际上,《六分钟英语》有一整期节目专门讲遗愿清单。

text: Imagine you're at the supermarket and find someone's shopping list.
zh: 想象一下,你在超市里发现了别人的购物清单。

text: This happens a lot to Jo Nolan, a linguistics researcher whose new book explores our love of lists.
zh: 这种事经常发生在Jo Nolan身上,她是一位语言学研究者,她的新书探讨了我们对清单的喜爱。

text: Here's Jo reading her shopping list on BBC Radio 4 programme Word of Mouth.
zh: 下面是Jo在BBC Radio 4节目《Word of Mouth》中朗读她自己购物清单的片段。

text: I only list the first four items, but the list read,
zh: 我只列出了清单上的前四项,内容是:

text: Nanas, aye -aye, puke, shrooms.
zh: 香蕉、指猴、呕吐、蘑菇。

text: Nanas, aye -aye, puke, shrooms?
zh: 香蕉、指猴、呕吐、蘑菇?

text: Can you work out what Joe was buying at the supermarket, Phil?
zh: Phil,你能猜出Jo在超市里要买什么吗?

text: OK, well, I'm going to guess that nanas are bananas and shrooms, they've got to be mushrooms, right?
zh: 好,我猜nanas是bananas(香蕉),shrooms肯定是mushrooms(蘑菇),对吧?

text: OK, but what about aye -aye and puke? What could they mean?
zh: 好,但aye-aye和puke是什么意思呢?

text: Well, here's Jo's explanation to Michael Rosen, presenter of BBC Radio 4's Word of Mouth.
zh: 来听听Jo向BBC Radio 4《Word of Mouth》主持人Michael Rosen做出的解释。

text: And let's just come back to puke. I mean, were you instructing yourself to be sick in the supermarket? Just a bit lost there. No, it's slightly grotesque.
zh: 我们再说说puke这个词。你是在超市里提醒自己要吐吗?我有点没搞明白。不,其实有点恶心。

text: My husband doesn't like cucumber and the abbreviation for cucumber would be puke, so he has always referred to it as puke.
zh: 我丈夫不喜欢黄瓜,而黄瓜(cucumber)的缩写正好是puke,所以他一直把黄瓜叫做puke。

text: Aha! Cucumbers make Joanna's husband puke – a slang word for vomit.
zh: 啊哈!原来黄瓜会让Joanna的丈夫“puke”——这是“呕吐”的俚语说法。

text: And puke rhymes with cuke, so it's a way of reminding her to buy cucumbers.
zh: 而且puke和cuke(黄瓜的简称)押韵,所以这其实是提醒她买黄瓜的方式。

text: Wow, sometimes lists are like secret codes.
zh: 哇,原来清单有时候就像密码一样。

text: In this episode, we'll find out why people love lists and learn some useful new words and phrases.
zh: 在这期节目中,我们将探讨人们为什么喜欢列清单,并学习一些实用的新单词和短语。

text: And remember, you'll find another list of all the vocabulary used on our website, bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 别忘了,我们网站bbclearningenglish.com上有本期用到的所有词汇清单。

text: But first, a question for you, Beth. According to the list on the Internet Movie Database website,
zh: 不过首先,Beth,我有个问题要问你。根据互联网电影数据库(IMDb)网站上的榜单,

text: which movie has made the most money ever?
zh: 哪部电影是有史以来票房最高的?

text: Is it… a Titanic b Avengers
zh: 是……a《泰坦尼克号》 b《复仇者联盟4:终局之战》

text: Endgame c Avatar
zh: 还是 c《阿凡达》?

text: I think it might be Avatar, although that might have been the most expensive to make.
zh: 我觉得可能是《阿凡达》,不过它可能是制作成本最高的那部。

text: I'm not sure. OK, well we'll find out at the end of the programme.
zh: 我也不确定。好,我们节目最后会揭晓答案。

text: Popular songs are full of lists, and one of the most famous is My Favourite Things,
zh: 流行歌曲里常常充满各种清单,其中最著名的一首就是《我最喜欢的东西》,

text: sung by Julie Andrews in the film The Sound of Music.
zh: 由朱丽·安德鲁斯在电影《音乐之声》中演唱。

text: The song lists all her favourite things, from raindrops on roses to whiskers on kittens.
zh: 这首歌列出了她喜欢的各种事物,从玫瑰上的雨滴到小猫的胡须。

text: Here, Joe Nolan and Michael Rosen discuss the song on BBC Radio 4 programme Word of Mouth.
zh: 下面,Jo Nolan和Michael Rosen在BBC Radio 4节目《Word of Mouth》中讨论了这首歌。

text: I feel like you get a big bang for your buck in that song.
zh: 我觉得这首歌真是物超所值。

text: Maybe it's the positivity, it's a bit saccharine, maybe that's also part of the issue.
zh: 也许是因为它传递的积极情绪,不过也有点甜腻,这可能也是问题所在。

text: The list encourages you to think really efficiently and economically.
zh: 这种清单式的写法会让人思维变得高效而简洁。

text: So I think you don't waste a lot of time and effort making a nice sentence or using long words.
zh: 所以你不会浪费太多时间和精力去雕琢句子或使用长词。

text: You just get to the nub of things quite quickly.
zh: 你能很快抓住事情的要点。

text: The song's list of favourite things is long, and Jo says you get a big bang for your buck –
zh: 这首歌里喜欢的事物清单很长,Jo说这真是‘物超所值’——

text: an idiom meaning that you gain a lot in return for the money or effort you spend on something. But not everyone likes the song.
zh: 这是一个习语,意思是你从花费的金钱或精力中获得了很大的回报。不过并不是每个人都喜欢这首歌。

text: Some call it saccharine – an adjective meaning excessively sweet, sentimental or insincere.
zh: 有人说它太saccharine(甜腻)了——这个形容词的意思是过分甜美、煽情或不真诚。

text: Lists often abbreviate the items they contain, so you get to the nub of things quickly.
zh: 清单常常会把内容简化,这样你能很快抓住要点。

text: The nub of something means the most important or essential point about it. Lists can be useful as well.
zh: ‘the nub’指的是某件事最重要或最核心的部分。清单也可以很实用。

text: Here's Jo Nolan giving an example of a useful list to BBC Radio 4's Word of Mouth.
zh: 来听听Jo Nolan在BBC Radio 4《Word of Mouth》节目中举的一个实用清单的例子。

text: A friend of mine who makes pros and cons lists finds that it really helps her resolve a situation.
zh: 我有个朋友喜欢列‘利弊清单’,她发现这真的能帮她解决问题。

text: She halves her page, pros, cons, and she said by the end of it, she knows what she's doing.
zh: 她把纸对半分,一边写优点,一边写缺点,她说写完之后,她就知道该怎么做了。

text: Lists help us make decisions by writing down all the pros and cons of a
zh: 清单能帮助我们做决定,方法就是把某种情况的所有利弊都写下来。

text: situation. The pros and cons is another
zh: ‘pros and cons’是另一种说法,

text: way of saying the advantages and disadvantages of something, especially when you're trying to choose between them.
zh: 指的是某件事的优点和缺点,尤其是在你需要做选择的时候。

text: The more you think about it, the more you see lists everywhere – in menus and bank bank statements, for example.
zh: 你越想就越会发现,清单无处不在——比如菜单和银行对账单。

text: And therapists even recommend listing all the good things in your life to feel happier.
zh: 心理治疗师甚至建议人们把生活中所有美好的事情列成清单,以此让自己更快乐。

text: Not bad for a few words on a scrap of paper. OK, it's time to reveal the answer to my question.
zh: 这么简单的几句话写在一张小纸片上,效果还真不错。好,现在是揭晓我问题答案的时候了。

text: Beth, I asked you which movie had made the most money ever and you said Avatar.
zh: Beth,我问你哪部电影是史上票房最高的,你说是《阿凡达》。

text: And now, according to the Internet Movie Database, that's exactly right.
zh: 根据互联网电影数据库的数据,答案完全正确。

text: Yay! OK, let's recap the vocabulary we've learned, starting with bucket list –
zh: 太好了!好,我们来回顾一下今天学到的词汇,首先是bucket list——

text: all the things you would like to do before you die. Puke is slang for vomit or sick.
zh: 指你在去世前想要完成的所有事情。puke是俚语,意思是呕吐或恶心。

text: If something gives bang for your buck, you gain a lot of extra value in return for the money or effort you put into it.
zh: 如果说某件事‘give you bang for your buck’,意思是你从付出的金钱或精力中获得了很大的价值回报。

text: If you call something saccharine, you criticise it for being too sweet or sentimental.
zh: 如果你说某件事是‘saccharine’,那是在批评它过于甜腻或煽情。

text: The nub of a problem or situation is the most important or essential thing about it.
zh: 一个问题或情况的‘nub’,指的是它最重要或最核心的部分。

text: And finally, something's pros and cons are its advantages and disadvantages.
zh: 最后,某事物的‘pros and cons’就是它的优点和缺点。

text: Once again, our six minutes are up, but remember you'll find a quiz and worksheet for this episode
zh: 我们的六分钟又到了尽头,别忘了在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上,

text: over on our website bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 能找到本期节目的测验和练习题。

text: Goodbye for now. Bye. dot com
zh: 再见啦。拜拜。dot com
